summary: The architecture plan demonstrates clear improvement over Round 2. The frozen requirement conflict has been resolved, and the DPIA execution workflow is now defined with ownership and milestones. All prior high-severity issues remain fixed. The architecture is implementation-ready, secure, and compliant in design, with only administrative DPIA documentation pending. The score improves modestly due to enhanced compliance alignment and internal consistency.
strengths: Resolved frozen requirement conflict by aligning retention policy with implemented configurable retention (default 12 months).
Defined DPIA execution workflow, ownership, and documentation milestones.
Maintained strong security posture with OAuth2/JWT, RBAC, and Azure-native controls.
Comprehensive observability, cost monitoring, and moderation governance.
Consistent modular design across frontend, backend, and data layers.
concerns: DPIA execution and documentation remain pending before production.
User-facing consent and retention configuration UX not yet fully described.
Operational cost and latency risks for multimodal features under high load.
recommendations: Complete and document the DPIA before production deployment.
Design and validate user-facing consent and retention configuration UX.
Conduct final compliance audit and DPIA verification before launch.
Maintain automated cost monitoring and moderation governance cadence.

issue_updates.title: Frozen requirement contradicts implemented retention policy
issue_updates.detail: Fixed: The plan now updates all references to reflect configurable retention (default 12 months) instead of indefinite retention. This removes the compliance traceability mismatch and restores internal consistency. The fix directly improved the internal_consistency and requirements_alignment scores.

issue_updates.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment execution pending
issue_updates.detail: Partially fixed: The plan now defines DPIA execution workflow, ownership, and documentation milestones, but actual DPIA completion and evidence remain pending. The issue severity is downgraded from high to medium since compliance readiness is now procedurally addressed but not yet finalized.

raw.thinking_summary: Round 3 audit confirms that the architect addressed both previously unresolved issues. The frozen requirement conflict (REQ_PRIVACY_CONFLICT) is now fixed by updating all plan references to configurable retention (default 12 months), aligning with GDPR/CCPA compliance. The DPIA_EXECUTION issue is partially fixed: execution workflow, ownership, and milestones are defined, but actual DPIA completion and documentation remain pending. All other issues remain resolved. The architecture maintains strong alignment, security, and operability, with improved internal consistency. The overall score improves slightly due to closure of the requirement conflict and partial DPIA progress.
